findings: Species identity is well supported: the plate shows the compact gray-brown Northern House Wren with a faint eyebrow, pale throat, thin slightly curved bill, fine dark barring on warmer brown wings and tail, short wings, cocked tail, and slender pinkish-gray legs.
Scientific name, family, and all three printed measurements match independent Cornell verification: 11–13 cm length, 10–12 g weight, and 15 cm wingspan.
The habitat, cavity-nesting, low-shrub/ground foraging, insect-and-spider diet, and bubbly song claims align with Cornell and Missouri Department of Conservation guidance.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears.
The central bird’s anatomy is coherent: one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail; the separate wing and head studies are clearly labeled anatomical details rather than additional birds.
